name: Large power transformers (LPT)
description: Large power transformers (≥100 MVA) step voltage between transmission and datacenter substations. Lead times have stretched from ~50 weeks (2020) to 200+ weeks (2026) as datacenter demand collides with grid-replacement cycles and a global manufacturing base that did not anticipate the load.
stackPosition: Grain-oriented electrical steel (Nippon Steel, POSCO, AK Steel/Cliffs) → core lamination → coil winding → vacuum impregnation → testing → ship by special transport → datacenter substation install. The OEM stage is the visible chokepoint but the upstream constraint is GOES (grain-oriented electrical steel) plus skilled coil-winding labor.

supplyStructure.name: Hitachi Energy
supplyStructure.note: Spun from ABB, part of Hitachi group

Siemens Energy Grid Technologies orders and lead-time commentary

companies.name: GE Vernova
companies.note: US-listed pure play on grid hardware; Electrification segment is the engine

companies.name: Siemens Energy
companies.note: Grid Technologies + gas turbines; combined power-equipment exposure

companies.name: Hitachi
companies.note: Hitachi Energy subsidiary is the global LPT volume leader

companies.name: Mitsubishi Electric
companies.note: Japan + US (TN) LPT capacity; growing US footprint

companies.name: HD Hyundai Electric
companies.note: Korea LPT exporter; US tariff exposure

companies.name: Eaton
companies.note: Adjacent electrical equipment; pad-mount / medium-voltage exposure

companies.name: Prolec GE
companies.note: JV between GE Vernova and Mexican firm Xignux; major US-market transformer supplier

insight: The transformer shortage is the most under-priced bottleneck in the AI capex chain because it is upstream of every announced datacenter MW. Hyperscalers can fund any GPU cost, but they cannot conjure a 345 kV transformer in under three years. The investable trade is the OEM long: GE Vernova as the US-listed pure play, Hitachi for those willing to take Japan exposure, Siemens Energy for European listing. The asymmetric kicker is grain-oriented electrical steel — a tiny supply chain (5-6 producers globally) where pricing power may exceed the transformer OEMs themselves. Watch for second-tier names (Toshiba Energy Systems, Hyosung Heavy) gaining qualification at US utilities as the majors run out of slots.

debates.question: Do US LPT lead times shorten below 150 weeks by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Modest improvement likely as GEV Charleroi and Hitachi US expansion ramp; unlikely to drop below 150 wk

debates.question: Does a US LPT supply Defense Production Act invocation occur by end of 2026?
debates.currentBelief: Discussed in working groups; political appetite real but legal mechanism unclear

debates.question: Does grain-oriented electrical steel become the binding constraint in 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Likely — GOES capacity additions trail transformer OEM capacity adds; spot pricing already signaling tightness
